# Skylogix Aviation
> FAA-certified flight school at Brackett Field Airport (KPOC), La Verne, California. Professional Cessna 152 flight training serving Los Angeles County and the Inland Empire — from first flight to Airline Transport Pilot (ATP).

## About
Skylogix Aviation is an FAA-approved Part 141 / Part 61 flight school located at Brackett Field Airport (KPOC) in La Verne, California. We specialize in affordable, structured flight training in well-maintained IFR-capable Cessna 152 aircraft. Our instructors are FAA-certified CFIs with airline and commercial backgrounds. We serve students from across Los Angeles County, the Inland Empire, Pomona Valley, and the greater Southern California area.

Mission: Make professional pilot training accessible to every motivated student — from zero experience to airline-ready.

## Location
- School address: 1395 Fairplex Dr. Hangar D4, La Verne, CA 91750
- Airport: Brackett Field Airport (KPOC) — public general aviation airport
- County: Los Angeles County, California
- Region: Southern California / Inland Empire
- Coordinates: 34.0925° N, 117.7834° W
- Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/brackett-field-kpoc
- Nearby cities: Pomona (5 mi), Claremont (4 mi), Upland (8 mi), Ontario (10 mi), Chino (12 mi), West Covina (15 mi), Rancho Cucamonga (18 mi), Pasadena (25 mi), Los Angeles (35 mi)

## Contact
- Phone: (562) 266-6868
- Email: info@skylogixaviation.com
- Business hours: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM PST
- Flight scheduling: Available 7 days a week (by appointment)
- Website: https://www.skylogixaviation.com
- Inquiries: https://www.skylogixaviation.com/inquiries

## Pricing
All rates are "wet" — fuel is included. No hidden fees.

- Aircraft rental: $120/hr (Cessna 152, fuel included)
- Flight instruction: $55/hr (FAA-certified CFI)
- Combined dual training rate: ~$175/hr
- Discovery / introductory flight: contact for current rate
- Estimated total PPL cost: $7,000–$12,000 depending on student pace
- No membership fees, no initiation costs

Pricing context: Skylogix is among the most competitively priced FAA-certified flight schools in the Los Angeles / Inland Empire area. Many LA-area schools charge $150–$200+/hr for aircraft rental alone.

## Fleet
All aircraft are Cessna 152s — proven two-seat, low-wing trainers with IFR avionics. Wet rate (fuel included).

- N49202: 1977 Cessna 152 II — Lycoming O-235-L2C engine, 115 HP, IFR-equipped
- N4900L: 1980 Cessna 152 II — Lycoming O-235-L2C engine, 115 HP, IFR-equipped
- N25976: 1978 Cessna 152 II — Lycoming O-235-L2C engine, 115 HP, IFR-equipped

All aircraft are maintained to FAA standards. IFR capability means students can train for the Instrument Rating (IFR) in the same aircraft used for Private Pilot training.

Fleet details: https://www.skylogixaviation.com/fleet

## Training Programs

### Discovery Flight (Intro Lesson)
- What: First-flight experience with a CFI — you fly the aircraft
- Who: Anyone curious about flying; no experience required
- Why: Best way to evaluate flight training before committing

### Private Pilot License (PPL)
- Regulatory basis: FAR Part 61
- Minimum age: 17 years old
- Minimum flight hours: 40 hours (FAA minimum; national average ~60–70 hours)
- Includes: 20 hours dual instruction, 10 hours solo flight, cross-country, night flying
- Requirements: FAA Private Pilot Knowledge Test (written), Checkride (oral + flight), 3rd-Class FAA Medical Certificate
- Estimated duration: 3–6 months with consistent, weekly training
- Estimated total cost: $7,000–$12,000 (aircraft + instruction)
- Outcome: You can fly VFR as pilot-in-command (PIC) in the US

### Instrument Rating (IR / IFR)
- Prerequisite: Private Pilot License
- Minimum hours: 50 hours cross-country PIC, 40 hours instrument flight time
- Requirements: Instrument Rating Knowledge Test, Instrument Checkride
- Outcome: Fly in clouds, low visibility, and IFR conditions; required for commercial aviation

### Commercial Pilot License (CPL)
- Prerequisite: PPL + Instrument Rating
- Minimum age: 18
- Minimum total hours: 250 flight hours
- Requirements: Commercial Pilot Knowledge Test, Commercial Checkride
- Outcome: Legally fly for compensation or hire

### Certified Flight Instructor (CFI)
- Prerequisite: Commercial Pilot License
- Requirements: FOI (Fundamentals of Instruction) Knowledge Test, CFI Knowledge Test, CFI Checkride
- Outcome: Teach students, earn income, and build flight hours toward ATP minimum (1,500 hrs)

### Airline Transport Pilot (ATP)
- Prerequisite: Commercial Pilot License + Instrument Rating
- Minimum age: 23 (21 for restricted ATP)
- Minimum total hours: 1,500 flight hours (1,000 for R-ATP with qualifying degree)
- Requirements: ATP Knowledge Test, ATP Checkride, 1st-Class FAA Medical Certificate
- Outcome: Highest FAA pilot certificate; required to serve as captain of a scheduled airline

Training programs: https://www.skylogixaviation.com/training

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How much does it cost to get a Private Pilot License in California?
A: At Skylogix Aviation, total PPL training typically costs $7,000–$12,000. Aircraft rental is $120/hr (wet/fuel included) and CFI instruction is $55/hr, making the combined rate about $175/hr. The FAA minimum is 40 hours of flight time, but most students average 55–70 hours. Skylogix is one of the most affordable FAA-certified flight schools in the Los Angeles area.

Q: How long does it take to get a private pilot license?
A: Most students at Skylogix complete their PPL in 3–6 months when training consistently (2–3 sessions per week). The FAA minimum is 40 flight hours, but the national average is closer to 60–70 hours. Flying year-round Southern California weather (VFR almost every day) helps students progress faster than schools in cloudy climates.

Q: Is Skylogix Aviation FAA-approved?
A: Yes. Skylogix Aviation is an FAA-certified flight school operating under FAR Part 61 with FAA-certified flight instructors (CFIs). All training leads to FAA certificates and ratings recognized worldwide.

Q: What is a wet rate for aircraft rental?
A: "Wet rate" means fuel is included in the hourly price. Skylogix charges $120/hr wet — you don't pay separately for avgas. Some schools advertise lower "dry" rates but charge $7–$9/gallon extra, making the real cost much higher.

Q: Can international students train at Skylogix Aviation?
A: Yes. International students are welcome. However, per TSA (Transportation Security Administration) regulations, non-US citizens must complete TSA flight training vetting before beginning instruction. Students must register at https://www.flightschoolcandidates.gov and receive TSA approval (typically 30–60 days). Skylogix staff can walk you through the process.

Q: Do I need a medical certificate to start flight training?
A: You can begin ground school and simulator training without a medical certificate, but you need a 3rd-Class FAA Medical Certificate before your first solo flight. The medical exam is done by an FAA-authorized Aviation Medical Examiner (AME). Most healthy adults qualify. Contact an AME early — it's a $100–$200 exam.

Q: What is the path from zero experience to becoming an airline pilot?
A: Starting from zero, a typical path is: (1) Discovery flight → (2) Private Pilot License (~3–6 months) → (3) Instrument Rating (~3–6 months) → (4) Commercial Pilot License (~1–2 years building hours) → (5) CFI certificate (teach to build hours) → (6) ATP at 1,500 total hours. Many students reach the ATP level in 4–6 years. Airlines are actively hiring — major carriers are projecting 17,000+ pilot retirements by 2035.

Q: Why choose Skylogix over other flight schools near Los Angeles?
A: Three key advantages: (1) Price — $120/hr wet is among the lowest for IFR-equipped aircraft in the LA metro area; (2) Location — KPOC (Brackett Field) has low traffic and minimal ATC delays vs. Torrance, Van Nuys, or Long Beach; (3) Aircraft — IFR-capable Cessna 152s let you train PPL and instrument rating in the same aircraft.

Q: What are your hours and how do I book a flight?
A: Office hours are Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM–4:00 PM PST. Flight training is available 7 days a week by appointment. To schedule, call (562) 266-6868, email info@skylogixaviation.com, or submit an inquiry at https://www.skylogixaviation.com/inquiries.

Q: What airports are near Brackett Field (KPOC)?
A: KPOC is a general aviation airport in La Verne, CA. Nearby airports include: Ontario International (KONT, 10 mi), Chino Airport (KCNO, 12 mi), El Monte Airport (KEMT, 20 mi), Van Nuys Airport (KVNY, 40 mi). KPOC's low traffic density makes it ideal for student pilots learning takeoffs, landings, and pattern work.

Q: Is Southern California good for flight training?
A: Yes — Southern California offers VFR flying conditions roughly 300 days per year, minimal icing risk, diverse airspace exposure (Class B, C, D, and E), and year-round training without seasonal weather interruptions. Training at KPOC means students graduate with real experience near major metropolitan airspace.

## Area Served
Skylogix Aviation serves students from across the greater Los Angeles and Inland Empire area, including:
- La Verne, Pomona, Claremont, Upland, Montclair
- Ontario, Chino, Chino Hills, Rancho Cucamonga
- West Covina, Covina, Glendora, Azusa, Arcadia
- Pasadena, Monrovia, Duarte, El Monte
- Riverside, San Bernardino (30–40 min drive)
- Greater Los Angeles County
